text: 100g of fat. polyunsaturated fat is one of the better parts of the profile. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. the rest of the section is nothing special. not relevant here.
0g of protein. everything else here is pretty flat. also not relevant here.
vitamin k at 11% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not adding much here.

on the plus side: helps meals actually work because a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one. small amounts can change a whole meal because that leverage matters when the fat quality is good. highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here.

the drawbacks next: calorie density is brutally high because tiny serving errors matter here. easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. extremely calorie dense by default because that is the unavoidable category burden.
A tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
